title: /sig
text: /sig Compile component signatures. /sig { /aft= DateTime } { /bef= DateTime } { /inf | /war } Switches /inf /war /aft= or /bef= Use With ide.exe —Uniface IDE Description Component signatures are generated and compiled when components are compiled. Use the /sig switch if it is necessary to recompile the signatures without recompiling the corresponding component. The location of compiled signatures is determined by the $RSO path. Related Links Compiling from the IDE Command Line The IDE command line can be used to compile multiple objects without opening the IDE. It is especially useful for automating compilation by means of batch scripts. Signatures A Uniface signature describes the remote interface of a component. The interface enables other components to address the component without knowing anything about the implementation. This allows components of different origin to communicate through their respective interfaces. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
